title: Lay up to a number you love
microFact: Don't advance the ball 'as far as possible' — lay up to your favorite full-wedge distance.
lesson:
Situation: par 5, your third can't reach the green. Most amateurs mindlessly smash their longest club — leaving an awkward 30m half-pitch, the hardest shot in golf.

The skill is LAYING UP TO A NUMBER: pick your favorite FULL-swing wedge distance (say 70–80m with your PW or gap wedge) and choose the layup club that leaves exactly that. A full swing has rhythm and a repeating result; a half-swing requires touch you haven't built yet.

Same logic when trouble crosses the fairway: short of a stream at 180m? Don't hit the 170m club ("maybe it stops") — hit the 150m club and take the water OUT of the hole entirely.

To run this system you need byte 49's homework — knowing your real carry distances — but even approximations transform your par 5s. Par 5s should be a high-handicapper's FRIENDS: no hero shots required, just three sensible hits and two putts for a personal-par 6, or better.

Caddie phrase that marks you as a thinker: "leave me 80 to the flag." Watch his eyebrows rise approvingly.

visual.caption: Lay up to a number: 210 out → hit the 130 club → full 80m wedge in.

quiz.question: You're 210m out on a par 5, can't reach. Your favorite wedge is a full 80m. Best play?
options: Longest club, get as close as possible
Putter along the ground for safety
A ~130m club, leaving your full 80m wedge
quiz.explanation: 210 − 130 = 80: your full-swing favorite. 'As close as possible' leaves the dreaded half-wedge.
